kept.text:
‘Hercules and the Cup of Helios’

Hercules, in the tenth of his twelve labours, was sent to the mythical island of Erythea to steal the cattle of the monstrous Geryon. On his long  journey, he was angered by the heat of the sun and so drew his bow against it. The God of the sun, Helios, was so impressed by his audacity that he gifted Hercules the great golden cup in which he returned each night to the east. In this cup Hercules sailed the ocean all the way to Erythea. In my painting, Hercules has landed on the island shore exhausted by his long voyage.

kept.text:
"Only those who have mastered both lion and eagle within are permitted to pass."
This is not fantasy — it’s code. Spiritual code.
And it’s not about mythical gates or external approval. It’s about the threshold within your own being. It means you cannot ascend, awaken, or embody your full power until you’ve unified the warrior and the seer — the lion and the eagle — within yourself.

The Lion Within You:
The lion is your raw power, your fire, your will.
It’s what gets you up when life knocks you down. It’s what protects your family, speaks truth without apology, and refuses to bow to lies or weakness.
But unchecked, the lion becomes aggression, pride, ego, and domination.
You’ve seen that in others. Maybe you’ve seen it in yourself. That’s the unrefined lion.
To master the lion is to embody strength with restraint. Courage without cruelty. Power without the need to prove it.

The Eagle Within You:
The eagle is your clarity, your vision, your higher mind.
It’s your ability to rise above drama, to see through illusion, to understand the patterns beneath the noise. It’s your intuition. Your inner knowing. The part of you that can read energy like a book.

But without grounding, the eagle becomes detachment, superiority, or paralysis by overthinking. Floating too high with no roots.
To master the eagle is to develop insight with humility. Vision that serves the whole, not just the self.
And when they unite...
You become something rare.

Grounded yet elevated. Fierce yet wise.
A force that’s not led by emotion, nor frozen by intellect — but guided by both.
That’s the Griffin.
That’s the Initiate.
That’s what it means to pass the gate.

How to begin embodying this:
Train your Lion – Face your fears. Speak the truth, even when your voice shakes. Develop discipline. Say no when you must. Set boundaries that protect what’s sacred.

Sharpen your Eagle – Meditate. Reflect. Study the patterns in your life. Learn to rise above reaction. Observe, before you respond. Listen to your intuition, even when logic can’t explain it.

Do the Work, Daily – This is not a weekend workshop or a motivational quote. This is a lifelong refinement. Pay attention to where you overuse one and neglect the other. Real alchemy is inner work.

Surround yourself with other Griffins in the making – This is why sacred groups exist. Iron sharpens iron. Wisdom echoes louder in communion.
You don’t need robes, temples, or secret handshakes to pass the threshold.
You just need truth in your heart, strength in your step, and clarity in your sight.
Unite the lion and eagle. Become the Griffin. And walk through the gate.

✨🙌🏽💫
